merge_stats.notes: Strong consensus on most content with clear semantic equivalence. GT2 provides more detailed coverage of discussion points. Few conflicts requiring review, mainly around specific wording differences and one decision assignee mismatch.

total_aligned_gt.meeting_context: Regular meeting of the City of Mountain View Visual Arts Committee held on November 9th, 2022, focused on approving prior minutes, hearing public comments, outlining upcoming agenda priorities, receiving CIP public art status updates, discussing remote meeting policy changes, refining processes for public art placement and selection, and reviewing the Center for the Performing Arts gallery call timeline.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Motion to approve October 12th, 2022 meeting minutes was approved unanimously by all committee members.
points.source: Jesse Cupp : OK, I'll make a motion to approve the minutes.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Staff provided contact information john.lang@mountainview.gov for artist questions and offered to provide background on committee processes.
points.source: John Lang : Please e-mail me and I'll give you some background on this and I'm happy to answer your questions. So it is my first name, John John dot Lang, that's Lang at mountainview.gov

points.speaker: Bruce England
points.text: Reported technical difficulties with PDF meeting codes that aren't embedded in Zoom links, making mobile access challenging.
points.source: Bruce England : And then on the, what I wanted to tell you is on the PDF and the PDF for the meeting it I found it difficult to use the code. The code's not embedded in the zoom like now.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Staff has requested direction from the City Clerk on permissible communications and will relay clear protocols to committee members when available.
points.source: John Lang : I have a inquiry into the Clerk's office on this matter and we'll once to make clear to me what the boundaries are, I will communicate that to the VAC.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Staff is developing comprehensive approach for downtown improvements including great separation, pedestrian mall, and other CIP projects as future agenda topic.
points.source: John Lang : So as far as upcoming items, one key item that was brought up and I'm working with our public work teammates on trying to bring forward a more comprehensive approach is all the elements associated with downtown and as it relates to public improvement.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Great Separation project for downtown will come to committee as early as December or February meeting, requiring call for artists sooner than later.
points.source: John Lang : And then the last one which is not on your list is the The Great Separation project for downtown. That will be possibly coming to as early as December, if not, it will be at your February meeting.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: City Council approved pilot program for public art integration with protected bike lane on California Street.
points.source: Jesse Cupp : I spoke to City Council and they made an exception and they're piloting it for the bike lane. I believe they voted and proved that there's going to be some public art in the protected bike lane that's going down California.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Aquatic Center project remains on track to open Fall 2023, currently over 25% complete with two of three contracts executed, with insurance requirements still being finalized.
points.source: John Lang : Once again, the project is still very much on track to open Fall 20, 23. It's a little over 25% complete. The one thing we are still continuing to work through is insurance requirements on a contract.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Fayette Park construction has commenced with no immediate updates beyond construction start.
points.source: John Lang : Their fight on Fayette Park construction just commenced. There is no other immediate update at this point in time.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Rankstorff Park maintenance and tennis building design near completion, construction bid expected end of year.
points.source: John Lang : And on the Rankstorff park maintenance and tennis buildings design is near completion now and we do anticipate bid, construction, bid going out here at the end of the year.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Mural artists for Rankstorff project working through insurance requirements, will present design concepts to committee after consulting senior and youth advisory committees.
points.source: John Lang : And we can continue to work with artists once again working through insurance requirements on contracts and hopefully getting those executed. So both of our mural artists are able to paint the murals as as envisioned.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: City Clerk directed all advisory committees to return to in-person meetings effective February 1, 2023, with City Council extending emergency orders through January.
points.source: John Lang : I minutes before this meeting started and for the good of the public, it appears the clerk has given direction to all the advisor committees that effective February 1, 2023, the advisory committees and City Council will be going back in person.

topics.topic: Public art placement and process improvements

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: 3D modeling will be available for larger CIP projects to help committee understand art placement relative to other park elements.
points.source: John Lang : So typically larger projects just out of size will typically have 3D modelling associated with that and that is something that can be brought and incorporated when reviewing larger projects

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Staff will provide better scale comparisons and encourage committee site visits, potentially as group visits with public works team.
points.source: John Lang : So as part of talking about projects, we'll start to use some better comparisons so people understand the scale and magnitude. The other suggestion was as part of the review process, all committee members are certainly invited and encouraged to visit the site.

points.speaker: Toot Too Thomson
points.text: Proposed standard checklist for art approval covering color, size, material, rotation, and height to ensure consistent communication with artists.
points.source: Toot Too Thomson : I think that's maybe they build a like a standard checklist. Like when we approve art, when we communicate art, just have a standard checklist item a color item B size, item C material item D rotation.

points.speaker: Clifford Bryant
points.text: Committee member suggested incorporating residents who participated in park equipment selection into public art selection process.
points.source: Clifford Bryant : Can we then build on that in and incorporate those public members into selection of the public art piece that'll go into their park. In other words invite them to see the pieces that are being discussed and get invite them to give comments to us

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Staff proposed quarterly check-ins during fabrication as contractual requirement, with documentation provided to committee at quarter, half, and three-quarter completion.
points.source: John Lang : So I think one way to potentially approach this maybe in the actual boilerplate contract language which requires say a quarterly like 1/4, a half a three quarter check in of either not to make it overly cumbersome

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Approved schematics need more detailed specifications including exact dimensions, color palettes, and materials to ensure contractual compliance.
points.source: John Lang : But to make sure that schematic, whatever it is that the committee approves, has that very well represented on that schematic 'cause it gets put as an attachment. So that's basically what we're getting and it's clear it's six feet, it's this set of colours etcetera, etcetera.

topics.topic: Center for the Performing Arts call

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Call for artists for 2023 CPA gallery display received 29 submissions, closing Saturday with jury process starting Sunday.
points.source: John Lang : So we we've had a call for artists out since October. On this coming Saturday that call will close. Currently we have 29 submissions, so starting Sunday you will be receiving some emails from CAFE to start to jury those submissions.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Committee will review submissions before December 13th meeting to select artists for approximately 5 exhibition slots with alternates.
points.source: John Lang : My hope is that between now and the December meeting, I'm hoping the VAC committee can spend some time before the December. I believe it's 13th meeting if I remember correctly reviewing those so we can at the next PAC meeting decide on the artist proposals.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Meeting adjourned at 7:40 PM with thanks to all participants.
points.source: Jesse Cupp : It's 740 and this meeting is adjourned. Thank you very much.

decisions.text: Staff will research historical dollar thresholds for alternative art selection processes and present options to committee for future decision-making.
decisions.source: John Lang : So I'll have to research that. So I'm happy to come back to the committee and say when in the past you've kind of done this other process where it's modelling, this is the level for those art pieces where this is kind of triggered.

decisions.text: Staff will bring public art strategy information to December meeting with next iteration details.
decisions.source: John Lang : I will be bringing back more information in December on this next iteration for you, but don't have it for you this evening.

decisions.text: Staff will provide call for artists link to committee members after meeting and clarify communication protocols with City Clerk's office.
decisions.source: John Lang : I will, I will forward the, I will forward this call out after this meeting to all the VAC. I have a inquiry into the Clerk's office on this matter and we'll once to make clear to me what the boundaries are.

decisions.text: Staff will develop comprehensive approach for downtown improvements including great separation and pedestrian mall as future agenda topic.
decisions.source: John Lang : So as far as upcoming items, one key item that was brought up and I'm working with our public work teammates on trying to bring forward a more comprehensive approach is all the elements associated with downtown

points.speaker: Toni Hsu
points.text: Minutes for the October 12, 2022 meeting were delivered to committee members and posted on the City Hall bulletin board; after offering public comment, the committee approved the minutes via roll call vote with unanimous yes responses.
points.source: Toni Hsu : Agenda item 3 minutes Approval minutes for the October 12th, 2022 meeting have been delivered to committee members and posted on the City Hall bulletin board.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Committee members present included Cliff Bryant, Susie Mehr, Regina Cycles, Tutu Thompson, Don Whitebread, Vice chair Hsu, with Chair Jesse Kopp arriving late.
points.source: John Lang : Committee member Cliff Bryant here. Susie Mehr. Thank you. Regina Cycles. Tutu Thompson. Thank you Don Whitebread. Here. Thank you and Vice chair Hsu. Here. And chair Jesse Kopp is currently not here.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: A public attendee reported difficulty connecting from agenda PDFs because Zoom codes were not embedded. Staff stated the issue has been raised with document processing and committed to work on resolving the inconsistency.
points.source: John Lang : I've raised it with document processing Bruce, and I'll work on getting it resolved, so thank you.

points.speaker: Naya
points.text: Local artist introduced herself as Mountain View High School 2019 graduate currently studying art and history in Rhode Island, interested in learning about public art processes.
points.source: Naya : I graduated from Mountain View High School in 2019, so 3-4 years ago or something like that at that point, this point. And I'm current students in Rhode Island studying art as well as history.

points.speaker: Naya
points.text: Proposed future project responding to El Camino bells that would create dialogue with existing monuments without toppling them, specifically near Castro Street bell.
points.source: Naya : So my idea kind of with this project is to make a piece which I have photos and things of which I could send to the committee later, that, yeah, it takes on like these kind of plays with the idea of a toppled monument, but without actually toppling the monument.

points.speaker: Susi Merhar
points.text: A committee member expressed support for VAC involvement in the pedestrian mall functional plan and emphasized interest in discussing the next public art strategy iteration, including initiatives, processes, and funding mechanisms.
points.source: Susi Merhar : …very happy to hear John that gonna work on a comprehensive approach towards the pedestrian mall… I hope we can talk about the public art strategy more, especially about the initiatives and how that all ties in and what processes and and funding will work.

points.speaker: Clifford Bryant
points.text: Requested agenda item to re-examine process for selecting public art in parks and better integration with play equipment selection.
points.source: Clifford Bryant : So this Cliff, one topic I'd like to that I mentioned the last meeting was kind of re looking at our process for selecting public art that goes into a public park and how that ties in with the art.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Given Clerk direction, the committee opted not to vote on the AB 361 resolution. The December meeting will occur virtually under Council's extension, and meetings will transition to in-person beginning in February.
points.source: Jesse Cupp : It's just gonna like, take… time… and not accomplish anything. So we're going to 7.2.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Committee will likely meet in atrium room which allows hybrid meetings, while Rankstorff Community Center lacks hybrid capability.
points.source: John Lang : The atrium is actually a hybrid room would allow for hybrid meetings. Unfortunately, the Rank Store Center does not allow for hybrid right at the present time.

points.speaker: Clifford Bryant
points.text: Committee member expressed concern about transition to in-person meetings affecting participation for members with families.
points.source: Clifford Bryant : Where it's been nice though is that it's been able to you know people with with families and whatnot to participate in the need these meetings and it's going to be more difficult for you know as a result.

topics.topic: Public art placement and process improvements

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Staff identified need for process improvements after Pyramid Park deployment, recognizing scale and proximity issues requiring new approach.
points.source: John Lang : So as committee member Bryant mentioned in upcoming Agenda topics, this was a topic I think that came up last committee meeting on really trying to understand after seeing Pyramid Park in the the deployment of the art, recognizing maybe there's some things need to change.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Playground equipment information will be shared with committee since it's typically already sourced before art selection, including color palette and design details.
points.source: John Lang : So making sure that that information is also conveyed from a colour palette from a look and feel. So as these projects go forward, typically playground structures are actually already either completely designed or already in the process of procurement.

points.speaker: Susi Merhar
points.text: Suggested pre-selecting larger pool of finalists to allow for detailed discussions about dimensions and placement before final selection.
points.source: Susi Merhar : In the sense of that we probably should pre select a larger pool and then narrow down just so that we can apply all those all that extra information and that we don't influence the artists beforehand

points.speaker: Toni Hsu
points.text: Committee emphasized importance of providing stipends when requesting multi-step process with additional work from finalist artists.
points.source: Toni Hsu : But we want some room and some some opportunity to work with the individual artists. And then we're so not asking. We're not only asking for their time, right, and their commitment, but we're also asking for them to essentially create.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Staff suggested threshold-based approach where smaller projects use straightforward process while larger projects employ iterative stipend-based selection.
points.source: John Lang : So maybe there's a threshold requirement because I think for the park that is extremely small where you're talking, it just minimally triggers the CIP project where it might just be benches. This iterative process seems a little and I think committee member White Bread was kind of on to this.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Committee agreed threshold should apply per art piece rather than total CIP project amount to allow appropriate process selection.
points.source: Jesse Cupp : But I'm thinking if there's a threshold, the threshold should probably apply for piece and not for the total amount of the CIP project.

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: Current process attaches artist's original submission to contract without additional schematics, with no iterative changes after City Council approval.
points.source: John Lang : So what ends up happening once the VAC approves, approves an art piece, it goes to City Council. But basically the submittal that the artist provided gets to be an attachment to the artist contract.

points.speaker: Clifford Bryant
points.text: Committee member noted discrepancies between approved submissions and final installed pieces, requesting checkpoint process during fabrication.
points.source: Clifford Bryant : What I've seen is that what's submitted to us and what we finally see installed may may differ and and and hence there's no and there's no checkpoint along the way.

topics.topic: Center for the Performing Arts call

points.speaker: John Lang
points.text: 2019 call for artists generated 41 respondents, providing context for current 29 submissions with three days remaining.
points.source: John Lang : Relative to last year the the call. Was. The the 2019 call, which was the last call that was done, had 41 respondents.

points.speaker: Toni Hsu
points.text: Committee member requested clarification on protocols for responding to direct public inquiries about Visual Arts Committee processes.
points.source: Toni Hsu : I have one question and I just I it would be helpful to know what the protocol is when general public reaches out to us directly with questions or comments or wanting to understand the inner workings on the visual arts Committee.

points.speaker: Jesse Cupp
points.text: Chair expressed confidence in staff leadership and thanked John Lang for keeping committee moving in right direction despite staff transitions.
points.source: Jesse Cupp : I think we're in really, really good hands with John, even though Tiffany's not here anymore. I think we got a great staff member who we can trust to keep things going in the right direction. So thanks, John.

decisions.text: Decline to take action on the AB 361 resolution, relying on Council's extension for December and planning for in-person meetings from February 1, 2023.
decisions.source: Jesse Cupp : It's just gonna… not accomplish anything. So we're going to 7.2.

decisions.text: Work with document processing to resolve inconsistent Zoom links in agenda PDFs.
decisions.source: John Lang : I've raised it with document processing Bruce, and I'll work on getting it resolved…

decisions.text: Staff will explore how to engage residents who participated in park equipment selection into public art selection process.
decisions.source: John Lang : Happy to explore. I I need to kind of understand the the parks process a little bit better but how the VAC can engage with that already identified group.

gt1_point.text: A member of the public (Naya) introduced herself as a local artist seeking to learn about public art; staff invited her to email for background and questions, offering direct contact for follow-up outside the meeting.
gt1_point.source: John Lang : Please e-mail me and I'll give you some background on this and I'm happy to answer your questions.
gt1_point.speaker: John Lang

gt2_point.text: Staff provided contact information john.lang@mountainview.gov for artist questions and offered to provide background on committee processes.
gt2_point.source: John Lang : Please e-mail me and I'll give you some background on this and I'm happy to answer your questions. So it is my first name, John John dot Lang, that's Lang at mountainview.gov
gt2_point.speaker: John Lang

needreview_gt.question: Are these two points describing the same interaction with Naya, or are they separate instances of John Lang offering contact information?
needreview_gt.merge_if_valid: Combine into single point about John Lang providing contact information to Naya and offering background on committee processes

gt1_point.text: The annual plan targets about five exhibits with two to three alternates. Due to contractual simplicity, only one artist will exhibit at a time, though combined shows may be considered in the future when staffing allows.
gt1_point.source: John Lang : So typically for the year it's at least five with two alternatives… From a contractual standpoint, it needs to be one at a time.
gt1_point.speaker: John Lang

gt2_point.text: Committee will review submissions before December 13th meeting to select artists for approximately 5-8 exhibition slots with alternates.
gt2_point.source: John Lang : My hope is that between now and the December meeting, I'm hoping the VAC committee can spend some time before the December. I believe it's 13th meeting if I remember correctly reviewing those so we can at the next PAC meeting decide on the artist proposals.
gt2_point.speaker: John Lang

needreview_gt.question: What is the correct number of exhibition slots - 5 with 2-3 alternates (GT1) or 5-8 slots with alternates (GT2)?
needreview_gt.merge_if_valid: Use the specific number confirmed in the transcript

gt1_point.text: Artists apply via CaFE; roughly three days remained as of the meeting. Staff will forward the call link to VAC for broader sharing. Older works are acceptable; there is no requirement that submissions be newly created.
gt1_point.source: John Lang : …three days and a couple hours left to apply… They can apply through cafe… I will forward the, I will forward this call out after this meeting to all the VAC.
gt1_point.speaker: John Lang

gt2_point.text: Call for artists for 2023 CPA gallery display received 29 submissions, closing Saturday with jury process starting Sunday.
gt2_point.source: John Lang : So we we've had a call for artists out since October. On this coming Saturday that call will close. Currently we have 29 submissions, so starting Sunday you will be receiving some emails from CAFE to start to jury those submissions.
gt2_point.speaker: John Lang

needreview_gt.question: Are these describing the same call timeline - GT1 says 3 days left, GT2 says closing Saturday. Which timing is accurate?
needreview_gt.merge_if_valid: Use the specific timeline confirmed in the transcript

gt1_point.text: Group site visits can be Brown Act compliant by noticing the meeting with purpose and time (e.g., on-site review of public art placement). Staff indicated this is an addressable process detail for future CIP art evaluations.
gt1_point.source: John Lang : …we just need to notice it as such and state exactly what is happening… we can certainly notice it as a meeting.
gt1_point.speaker: John Lang

needreview_gt.question: Is this point about Brown Act compliance for site visits substantive enough to include, or is it procedural detail that GT2 appropriately omitted?
needreview_gt.merge_if_valid: Include in single_aligned_gt if confirmed as substantive discussion point

gt1_point.text: Notify VAC when CPA jurying opens and request members review submissions before December 13.
gt1_point.source: John Lang : …starting Sunday you will be receiving some emails from CAFE to start to jury… before the December 13th meeting…

gt2_point.text: Committee will review 29 CPA gallery submissions before December 13th meeting to select artists for 2023 exhibitions.
gt2_point.source: John Lang : My hope is that between now and the December meeting, I'm hoping the VAC committee can spend some time before the December. I believe it's 13th meeting if I remember correctly reviewing those

needreview_gt.question: Who has the responsibility - John Lang to notify (GT1) or Committee members to review (GT2)? Are these separate actions or the same decision with different assignee interpretation?
needreview_gt.merge_if_valid: Clarify whether this is John Lang's action to notify or Committee's action to review, or separate complementary decisions
